task_updates.executor_notes: Added DatabaseHandler force-reconcile primitives that intentionally bypass the normal strict transition guards only for authoritative on-chain correction. They accept submitted/processing/failed/manual_hold, reject pending_confirmation/queued/cancelled through the unchanged guard path, append reconcile_confirmed/reconcile_failed tx_signature_history entries, and emit WARNING logs with before/after status. Dedicated reconciliation tests cover allowed starts, rejected starts, history append, WARNING logging, and unchanged mark_payment_* strictness.

Implemented dynamic Solana priority-fee selection inside the shared SolanaClient. `_get_dynamic_priority_fee` now uses `getRecentPrioritizationFees` via SDK method when available and solders RPC request fallback otherwise, computes the 75th percentile of non-zero fees, clamps to the configured floor/ceiling, logs WARNING and falls back to the static floor on error, and `send_sol` now uses/logs the chosen value. Tests cover percentile math, floor clamp, ceiling clamp, fallback behavior, and send_sol wiring.

Implemented admin alert fallback in the post-cleanup PaymentWorkerCog. The cog now reads `ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID`, both success and failure admin DM paths route through `_deliver_admin_alert`, the helper falls back to `safe_send_message` on `discord.Forbidden` or HTTP 429, and it logs ERROR when neither DM nor fallback delivery succeeds. Tests cover helper routing, Forbidden-triggered fallback, and double-failure ERROR logging.

Added `scripts/check_payment_invariants.py` as a daily read-only payment audit. It implements all five requested checks, prints a structured JSON report, exits non-zero on hard violations, verifies confirmed Solana rows against `getSignatureStatuses`, checks stale pending/processing rows, verifies wallet test-payment provenance, detects wallet-address reuse, and evaluates the 24h admin payout cap. Synthetic-fixture tests cover each invariant and the non-zero `main()` path. No CI workflow or scheduler config was added.

Added structured `tx_confirm_decision` logger.info events to all three `SolanaClient.confirm_tx` branches: confirmed, not_found, and errored. The log payload includes `event`, `signature`, `err`, `slot`, `confirmation_status`, and `decision`. Tests assert the structured extra payload for each branch without changing confirmation semantics.

Added `ReconcileDecision` and `PaymentService.reconcile_with_chain(...)` in the post-cleanup PaymentService. The method now covers `reconciled_confirmed`, `reconciled_failed`, `allow_requeue`, `keep_in_hold`, and `not_applicable`, uses `now - submitted_at` for the 150s blockhash heuristic, and treats provider exceptions or `rpc_unreachable` as `keep_in_hold` without mutating state. Unit coverage in `tests/test_payment_reconcile.py` exercises all required branches.

Added `db_handler.get_legacy_provider_payment_requests(guild_ids)`, `PaymentService.migrate_legacy_provider_rows(guild_ids)`, and the worker startup hook in `PaymentWorkerCog.cog_load`. The migration rewrites `grants -> solana_grants`, `admin_chat -> solana_payouts`, and marks unknown producers `manual_hold` with the requested reason while logging one WARNING per legacy row. Tests cover the DB selector, the three migration branches, and the one-time worker startup call.

Threaded `bot.payment_service` through the admin-chat retry/release tools and gated both actions on `reconcile_with_chain(...)` before any DB mutation. Both tools now fail closed when `payment_service` is missing, return reconciled truth for `reconciled_confirmed`/`reconciled_failed`, hold on `keep_in_hold`, and only mutate on `allow_requeue`. Verification covered direct tool calls plus dispatcher wiring via `execute_tool(...)` in the full module test run.

Added the slash-only `/payment-resolve` command to `PaymentUICog` with the admin gate enforced inside the callback body using `ADMIN_USER_ID`. The callback reports `decision`, `reason`, current row `status`, and a redacted signature in an ephemeral code block. Verification covers non-admin rejection and all reconcile decision branches. A grep over `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` for `payment_resolve|execute_payment_resolve` returned no matches, preserving the no-LLM-surface constraint.

Added `rpc_unreachable` handling in `SolanaProvider` and bridged it into `PaymentService` recovery/confirmation paths. Connection-family lookup failures now return `rpc_unreachable`, while a legitimate confirmation wait-budget timeout still returns `timeout`. Both `_confirm_submitted_payment(...)` and `recover_inflight(...)` now manual-hold with `rpc_unreachable: confirmation RPC offline`, and existing `reconcile_with_chain(...)` already keeps such rows in hold from T2. Verification includes provider sentinel tests plus service-level tests for both call sites.

Refactored `PaymentService.request_payment(...)` into a thin orchestrator that now delegates to exactly five helpers: `_normalize_inputs`, `_detect_collision_and_idempotent_row`, `_derive_amounts`, `_enforce_caps`, and `_persist_row`. The public signature stayed unchanged, the existing log messages were preserved as code motion rather than rewritten behavior, and the refactor held under both the targeted payment-module run and the full pytest suite without needing refactor-specific test edits.

task_updates.executor_notes: The investigation separated the DB claim semantics from the real startup overlap. `claim_due_payment_requests` remains structurally safe at the `queued -> processing` boundary under `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, but the worker loop could begin before `recover_inflight()` finished and re-scan fresh `processing/submitted` rows during startup. I added the specified 150-second reclaim guard in `PaymentService.recover_inflight()` and a logical-ordering sanity-check test in `tests/test_payment_race.py`; the new test and the full suite both pass.
commands_run: pytest tests/test_payment_reconcile.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_social_route_tools.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_payment_race.py tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --tb=no -q --no-header

task_updates.executor_notes: Reproduced the same-recipient admin collision at the downstream flow using two distinct `source_channel_id` values so the intent-dedupe layer was bypassed on purpose. The fix is the required millisecond-precision `producer_ref` in `execute_initiate_payment(...)`, and the regression test drives both intents through `_start_admin_payment_flow` to verify they persist as separate payments. The targeted payment/admin modules and the full suite pass with the fix in place.

task_updates.executor_notes: Ran the final verification sweep end to end. Phase B validation passed (`30 passed`), Phase C validation passed (`6 passed`), and the full suite passed green (`160 passed, 2 warnings`). The required Phase D command with `--hypothesis-show-statistics` was attempted exactly as specified and failed because this environment's pytest does not recognize that flag; I then reran the same modules without the flag and they passed (`3 passed`). I inspected the verdict comments directly: `tests/test_payment_race.py` cites `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` plus Postgres read-committed isolation and labels the test a logical-correctness sanity check, and `tests/test_admin_payments.py` clearly states the T13 collision was reproduced and fixed via millisecond `producer_ref`. For the invariants script, an exact local seeded DB run was not available in this environment, so I used a throwaway Python harness that imported `scripts/check_payment_invariants.py`, injected synthetic violating rows into its `main(...)` entrypoint, and confirmed a nonzero exit (`1`). I also wrote a throwaway reconcile-with-chain harness that drove a failed row through `PaymentService.reconcile_with_chain(...)`, verified `reconciled_confirmed`, verified the row status changed to `confirmed`, and verified a `reconcile_confirmed` tx-signature-history append. Both throwaway scripts were deleted and their deletion was verified.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified both force-reconcile primitives accept only `submitted`/`processing`/`failed`/`manual_hold`, reject `pending_confirmation`/`queued`/`cancelled`, append `reconcile_*` tx_signature_history entries, and log at WARNING. The original `mark_payment_confirmed`/`mark_payment_failed` guards were left unchanged and are covered by regression assertions.

Verified `_get_dynamic_priority_fee` computes the 75th percentile of non-zero fees, clamps to `[floor, ceiling]`, falls back to the static floor with a WARNING on exceptions, and is wired into `send_sol`. Tests cover percentile math, floor clamp, ceiling clamp, fallback, and send-path usage.

Verified `_deliver_admin_alert` tries DM first, falls back to `ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID` on `discord.Forbidden` or HTTP 429, logs ERROR when both paths fail, and both success/failure admin DM flows now route through the helper.

Verified `scripts/check_payment_invariants.py` implements checks (a)-(e), exits non-zero on hard violations, and has synthetic-fixture coverage for each invariant. No CI workflow or scheduler file was added.

Verified `SolanaClient.confirm_tx` emits structured `tx_confirm_decision` logs for confirmed, not_found, and errored branches, and the test module asserts the expected extra-dict shape for each branch.

Verified `reconcile_with_chain` covers `reconciled_confirmed`, `reconciled_failed`, `allow_requeue`, `keep_in_hold`, `not_applicable`, and `rpc_unreachable -> keep_in_hold` with dedicated unit coverage. The 150s blockhash heuristic is implemented as `datetime.now(timezone.utc) - submitted_at`.

Verified `migrate_legacy_provider_rows` handles all three branches (`grants -> solana_grants`, `admin_chat -> solana_payouts`, `unknown -> manual_hold`), and `PaymentWorkerCog.cog_load` now calls the migration once at startup. Fake-DB tests cover both the migration behavior and the startup hook.

Verified the dispatcher now passes `bot` for both `retry_payment` and `release_payment`, both tools fail closed when `payment_service` is missing, and `reconcile_with_chain(...)` runs before any DB mutation. Module tests cover missing service plus `reconciled_confirmed`, `reconciled_failed`, `allow_requeue`, and `keep_in_hold` branches for both tools, along with dispatcher routing.

Verified `/payment-resolve` checks `ADMIN_USER_ID` inside the callback body, reports all reconcile decision branches through the UI callback tests, and `rg -n "payment_resolve|execute_payment_resolve" src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` returned no matches.

Verified `rpc_unreachable` is emitted only from connection-family failures in `SolanaProvider`, while a responsive confirmation wait-budget timeout still returns `timeout`. Both `_confirm_submitted_payment(...)` and `recover_inflight(...)` now branch to the distinct `rpc_unreachable: confirmation RPC offline` manual-hold reason, and `reconcile_with_chain(...)` already treats `rpc_unreachable` as `keep_in_hold` from T2.

Verified `request_payment(...)` now delegates to `_normalize_inputs`, `_detect_collision_and_idempotent_row`, `_derive_amounts`, `_enforce_caps`, and `_persist_row`, with the public signature unchanged. The refactor survived both the targeted payment-module run and the full pytest suite, which is the regression gate for the byte-identical behavior claim.

Verified the verdict comment at the top of `tests/test_payment_race.py` cites `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` and Postgres read-committed isolation as the DB-level proof boundary, explicitly labels the test a logical-correctness sanity check, and does not contain the forbidden phrase `proven safe by this test`.

Verified the D11 reproduction uses distinct `source_channel_id` values and drives the downstream admin payment flow, not the intent-dedupe layer. The fix in `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py` uses `int(time.time() * 1000)` rather than a UUID suffix, and the regression test carries a verdict comment stating the collision was reproduced and fixed.

Verified `hypothesis` was added to `requirements.txt`, the `RuleBasedStateMachine` includes a `reconcile_with_chain` rule, and the file defines all five invariants. The terminal-state invariant only blocks backward transitions after `confirmed/cancelled`, so reconciliation from `failed/manual_hold` is intentionally not treated as a backward terminal transition.

Verified `PaymentService.__init__` now raises `ValueError` below `MIN_TEST_LAMPORTS = 2_000_000`, `main.py` keeps the `PAYMENT_TEST_AMOUNT_SOL` env read while removing the duplicate startup guard, and `tests/test_payment_reconcile.py` covers the `0.0001` failure case explicitly.

Verified `redact_wallet` is defined exactly once in `src/common/redaction.py`; `payment_service.py`, `payment_worker_cog.py`, `payment_ui_cog.py`, and `db_handler.py` now import it via alias; and the admin-chat-specific `_redact_wallet_address`, `_redact_wallet_row`, and `_redact_payment_row` helpers remain untouched. The legacy `payment_cog.py` file is absent in this post-cleanup tree, so the worker/UI split is the correct import surface here.

Verified `docs/payments.md` includes the state machine ASCII diagram with reconciliation edges, the current `PaymentActorKind x producer` table derived from `src/features/payments/producer_flows.py`, the reconciliation contract explaining why `force_reconcile_*` intentionally bypasses normal guards and that reconciliation is only reachable via `/payment-resolve`, and code/SQL file:line pointers throughout. The file is 303 lines long.

Verified `docs/runbook-payments.md` covers all six required scenarios (`manual_hold`, `failed`, ghost-verified wallet, DM undelivered, RPC down, budget cap), includes a scheduler wire-up section explicitly marked `OPERATOR DECISION`, and no CI workflow or Railway config file was added as part of this batch.

Verified the full pytest suite passed green (`160 passed, 2 warnings`), the Phase D verdict comments explicitly cite Postgres semantics for T12 and clearly report the T13 collision-fix outcome, the invariants audit path was proven to exit nonzero on synthetic violations via a throwaway harness around `scripts/check_payment_invariants.py`, and the temporary reconcile/audit verification scripts were deleted afterward.
